title: KAMAK tree conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
context_text: Hons, Klimošová, Kucheriya, Mikšaník, Tkadlec, and Tyomkyn studied which digraphs must appear as a subgraph in every digraph of sufficiently large minimum out-degree, showed that all such digraphs must be oriented forests with a specific (grounded) structure, and conjectured that vice-versa every grounded forest is $\delta^{+}$-enforcible. The conjecture reduces to the case of oriented trees (connected components), since disjoint copies can be embedded sequentially.
attributed_to: Hons, Klimošová, Kucheriya, Mikšaník, Tkadlec, and Tyomkyn
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full: every grounded forest is $\delta^{+}$-enforcible, i.e., it appears as a subdigraph in every digraph of sufficiently large minimum out-degree.
paper_title: Proof of the KAMAK tree conjecture
